tasks.title: Optimize Google Business Profile for local buyers
tasks.phase: Phase 1 · Tier 1 · Warmest first-sale channel (this week)
tasks.notes: Warmest first-sale channel. Lock in categories (screen printing / embroidery / uniform store), real work photos, services list, Bridgeport CT service area.

prep.what: Optimize the store's Google Business Profile so local search impressions convert into calls and visits.
prep.understand: GBP categories, photos, and service area directly determine local-pack rankings. 'Screen printing near me' is a map-pack result — GBP IS the product for local search, not the website.
approach: Log into business.google.com with the store account.
Set primary category to Screen Printing; add Embroidery Service + Uniform Store as secondary categories.
Upload 10+ real work photos (finished shirts, embroidery close-ups, the shop floor) — Google weights photo recency.
Fill in the services list and set service area to Bridgeport CT + surrounding towns (Fairfield, Stratford, Milford).
resources: business.google.com
Google Business Profile Help Center
Phone camera roll / past job photos

tasks.title: Reviews engine — collect reviews + render stars sitewide
tasks.notes: Outreach to past customers for reviews; wire aggregateRating so they render as stars. t2 schema already deployed.

prep.what: Run outreach to collect the first 5-10 Google reviews and verify the star schema is pulling real data.
prep.understand: The aggregateRating JSON-LD (deployed from t2) tells Google to show stars on search snippets. Stars lift click-through rate ~35%. Even 5-10 reviews pushes you ahead of most local competitors who have none.
approach: Pull your last 10-15 completed orders and identify happy customers (recent, no complaints).
Send a short personal text or email: 'Hey [name], glad you loved the shirts — would you mind leaving us a quick Google review? [link] Takes 30 sec.' No incentives.
Open the t2 schema code and verify `aggregateRating.ratingCount` reads from a live data source, not a hardcoded number.
If hardcoded, note the current value and plan to update it as reviews arrive — or wire to a DB field updated weekly.
resources: Google Business Profile short review link (from GBP dashboard under 'Get more reviews')
The t2 schema code already merged into the codebase
Order records / customer contact list

tasks.title: Sitewide OG share image + root-layout hreflang + home/uniforms title polish
tasks.notes: Create 1200×630 OG card (needs design input), wire into root layout + twitter card tags; add keyword+location home title.

prep.what: Design the OG share card and wire it into the site — the thumbnail shown whenever anyone shares a link on social, iMessage, or Slack.
prep.understand: Open Graph `og:image` must be 1200×630px, under 1 MB. Without it every link preview is blank. `hreflang` signals English-US locale to Google. Home title polish means switching to keyword+location format: 'Custom Screen Printing & Embroidery | Bridgeport, CT'.
approach: In Canva or Figma: logo on a brand background, store name + short tagline + 'Bridgeport, CT'. Keep it simple — it renders at 2 inches wide in most previews.
Export as PNG (1200×630, under 1 MB), place in `/public/` as `og-image.png`.
In root layout, add `<meta property="og:image" content="/og-image.png">` and `<meta name="twitter:image" content="/og-image.png">`.
Add `<link rel="alternate" hreflang="en-US">` in root layout; update the home `<title>` to the keyword+location format.
resources: Canva.com or Figma for the card design
OG Image spec: 1200×630px, under 1 MB
Root layout file where t11 wired og:title and og:description

tasks.title: Google Ads micro-budget on high-intent local terms
tasks.notes: $10-20/day on terms already ranking #1-3 organically. Optional — costs money, Haniel's call.

prep.what: Launch a Google Search Ads campaign at $10-20/day targeting the same local buyers already finding you organically.
prep.understand: These terms are proven demand (you're already #1-3 organically). Paid ads appear in the top slots ABOVE organic — so you own both positions on the same results page. Only downside is budget; pause anytime.
approach: Go to ads.google.com → New campaign → Search → Website visits or Leads.
Target: Bridgeport CT + 20-mile radius. Keywords (phrase or exact): 'custom shirts for business', 'screen printers near me', 'uniform printing near me'.
Write 2-3 responsive ad headlines mixing keyword + location + CTA ('Screen Printing Near Bridgeport | Fast Turnaround | Get a Quote').
Set daily budget $10-15, Maximize Clicks. Review after 7 days; pause keywords that spend without converting.
resources: ads.google.com
Google Keyword Planner for bid and volume estimates
Google Ads Help Center — Responsive Search Ads guide

tasks.title: Brand: indigo/slate palette + logo assets + Company Profile settings section
tasks.notes: Brand/Company-Profile trailing in Phase 0. Foundation for PDF letterhead and sidebar.

prep.what: Install the indigo/slate brand palette, wire in the AZ logo, and build the Company Profile settings page.
prep.understand: This is the visual foundation everything inherits from — PDFs (built next in t19), email templates, and the sidebar all pull logo + company name from here. Do this first so t19 has real letterhead to render.
approach: In `tailwind.config.js`, extend `colors` with brand tokens: `brand.primary: indigo-600`, `brand.hover: indigo-700`, `surface.base: slate-900`, `surface.muted: slate-800`.
Add the AZ Team AI logo (SVG + PNG) to `/public/images/`; swap out any placeholder in the sidebar header component.
Create a Company Profile settings section (reuse the existing settings infrastructure from the Module-project clone): fields for company name, address, phone, contact email, and logo upload.
Verify the sidebar renders the new logo and the settings form saves/loads correctly before moving on.
resources: tailwind.config.js
Existing Settings model/controller from Module-project (the clone base)
AZ Team AI logo file

tasks.title: PDF pipeline (DomPDF invoice template + AZ letterhead, shared Money)
tasks.notes: DomPDF invoice template with AZ letterhead; shared Money formatter for cent-to-dollar display.

prep.what: Build the DomPDF invoice PDF — the actual document clients receive when an invoice is sent.
prep.understand: DomPDF renders a Blade HTML template to a PDF binary inside Laravel. The template needs the AZ letterhead (logo + company info from t7), invoice metadata, a line-items table, and totals. The shared `Money` utility converts integer cents to formatted dollar strings — used everywhere money is displayed.
approach: Create `resources/views/pdf/invoice.blade.php`: letterhead block (logo + company name/address from Company Profile settings), invoice meta (number, issue date, due date, client name/address), line-items table (description, qty, unit price, line total), subtotal/tax/grand-total footer, and payment instructions.
Add or reuse a `Money` helper: `Money::format(int $cents): string` returning '$1,234.56'. Check if one already exists from the recurrence engine (t16) or totals service (t24) — don't duplicate.
Create `InvoicePdfService::generate(Invoice $invoice): string` — loads the Blade view with invoice data, calls DomPDF to render, returns PDF bytes.
Test: generate from a seeded invoice and visually verify the letterhead, line items, and totals render without overflow or misalignment.
resources: `barryvdh/laravel-dompdf` package docs
ct-pros-sys PDF pattern for reference (QBInvoiceSync and InvoicePdfService)
Company Profile settings data built in t7 above

tasks.title: Dashboard AR cluster + cashflow calendar
tasks.notes: In progress. Design done (Phase 1 t8). AR-aging backend done (Phase 2e t44 Module C). Wire the frontend now.

prep.what: Wire the AR tiles (outstanding/overdue/current) and cashflow calendar onto the main dashboard — the live financial heartbeat panels.
prep.understand: AR cluster = 3 stat tiles: total AR outstanding, overdue (past due date), current (not yet due). Cashflow calendar = compact month-grid with invoice due dates as chips. Design is finalized from Phase 1 (t8). The AR-aging backend query is live from Phase 2e Module C (t44). You're wiring the Inertia/React frontend to it.
approach: Find the AR aging controller/route from Phase 2e t44 Module C. Confirm it returns: total outstanding, overdue, current, and a breakdown by aging bucket (0-30 / 30-60 / 60-90+ days).
Build the AR cluster React component: 3 stat tiles using existing tile primitives from Phase 1, plus a mini aging breakdown bar at the bottom.
Build the cashflow calendar: a compact month grid where each day shows a colored chip if invoices are due. Clicking a chip surfaces a popover or routes to the invoice list filtered by that date.
Drop both into the Dashboard hub page and confirm data loads on navigation.
resources: Phase 1 dashboard hub design notes (t8 in snapshot)
AR aging endpoint from Phase 2e t44 Module C (Reporting/KPIs)
Existing Inertia page and component structure in the codebase
